primary_segment: Uranium mining (in-situ recovery) and trading
notes: World's largest uranium producer (~20% of global mined supply); majority owned via Samruk-Kazyna sovereign fund; also dual listed on AIX (Astana). ISR mining keeps output relatively routable, so not judged a chokepoint despite scale.

primary_segment: Uranium mining and UF6 conversion
notes: Major Western miner (McArthur River/Key Lake, Cigar Lake, Saskatchewan) and UF6 converter (Port Hope/Blind River, Ontario); also trades as CCO on TSX. Holds 49% of Westinghouse (with Brookfield, acquired 2023) and co-owns Global Laser Enrichment with Silex Systems.

primary_segment: Full fuel-cycle services: conversion, enrichment, recycling
notes: French state holds roughly 90%; unlisted. One of only four global enrichers (Georges Besse II, Tricastin) and a top conversion operator (Malvesi/Comurhex, Philippe Coq); also operates La Hague, the world's largest commercial spent-fuel reprocessing plant.

primary_segment: Uranium enrichment services and enriched-uranium export trading
notes: Russian state atomic energy corporation; TENEX is its enrichment sales/export arm. Russia holds roughly 40-46% of global enrichment capacity, the single largest chokepoint in the cycle; partially restricted by the US Prohibiting Russian Uranium Imports Act (2024, waivers running to 2028).

primary_segment: Nuclear fuel assembly fabrication, including VVER fuel
notes: Rosatom's fuel-fabrication division. Long the sole qualified supplier of VVER-440/1000 fuel assemblies for Soviet-design reactors across Eastern Europe; Westinghouse and Framatome only began qualifying alternative VVER fuel after 2022.

primary_segment: Uranium mining (Kazakh JV assets; historic US ISR)
notes: Rosatom's mining arm (via ARMZ Uranium Holding); holds joint-venture stakes in Kazakh ISR mines alongside Kazatomprom. Mining judged more routable than enrichment or fuel fabrication.

primary_segment: Uranium enrichment services
notes: Private consortium owned by the UK government, the Dutch state, and German utilities RWE/E.ON (via Uranit); unlisted. One of the 'big four' enrichers, with centrifuge plants in the UK (Capenhurst), Germany (Gronau), Netherlands (Almelo) and US (Eunice, New Mexico), the only major non-Russian, non-Chinese enrichment base at scale.

primary_segment: Vertically integrated state nuclear fuel cycle and reactor operator
notes: State-owned parent, unlisted (listed reactor subsidiary China National Nuclear Power trades as 601985 on the Shanghai Stock Exchange). Fourth member of the global enrichment oligopoly alongside Rosatom, Urenco and Orano, though most capacity serves domestic Chinese reactor build-out; fully vertically integrated from mining through reprocessing.

primary_segment: Uranium trading/mining JV interests; parent CGN operates reactors
notes: Parent China General Nuclear Power Corporation is China's largest reactor operator. CGN Mining, the HK-listed offshoot, holds minority equity stakes in Kazakh ISR uranium joint ventures (Semizbay-U, Irkol) to secure feedstock.

primary_segment: PWR/AP1000 reactor technology and fuel fabrication
notes: Private, owned by a Brookfield Renewable / Cameco consortium since 2023. Leading Western PWR fuel and AP1000 reactor vendor; now qualifying VVER-1000 fuel as a non-Russian alternative for Ukraine and Eastern Europe, chipping at TVEL's chokepoint.

primary_segment: PWR/EPR reactor technology and fuel fabrication
notes: Majority owned by EDF (~80.5%), with Mitsubishi Heavy Industries and Assystem minority stakes; unlisted. Builds EPR reactors and fabricates fuel at Romans-sur-Isere; competes with Westinghouse in Western PWR fuel, keeping that layer more routable than enrichment.

primary_segment: Uranium enrichment, including HALEU
notes: Only US-owned enricher with operating centrifuges (American Centrifuge Plant, Piketon, Ohio); sole current US domestic HALEU producer under DOE contract, central to the advanced-reactor fuel bottleneck.

primary_segment: Naval reactor fuel/components, medical isotopes, expanding commercial nuclear
notes: Sole fabricator of US Navy reactor fuel; growing commercial and microreactor fuel ambitions (including TRISO fuel work), but not yet a commercial-fuel chokepoint.

primary_segment: US in-situ recovery uranium mining and physical uranium holding
notes: Largest US-focused uranium developer/producer (Texas, Wyoming ISR hubs); also stockpiles physical U3O8.

primary_segment: Uranium development (Athabasca Basin, Rook I project)
notes: Developing what could become one of the world's largest high-grade uranium mines (Rook I, Saskatchewan); pre-production as of 2026. Also trades on NYSE.

primary_segment: Uranium mining (Langer Heinrich, Namibia)
notes: Restarted the Langer Heinrich mine in Namibia in 2024 after a multi-year care-and-maintenance suspension. Also trades on TSX.

primary_segment: Uranium development (Wheeler River, Athabasca Basin)
notes: Advancing the Phoenix in-situ recovery deposit at Wheeler River, one of the highest-grade undeveloped uranium projects globally. Also trades on NYSE American.

primary_segment: US conventional uranium milling (White Mesa, Utah) and rare-earth processing
notes: Operates the only fully licensed conventional uranium mill in the US; White Mesa also produces separated rare earths and is exploring HALEU feedstock and vanadium recovery. Also trades on TSX.

primary_segment: Laser-based uranium enrichment (SILEX process); depleted-tails re-enrichment
notes: Joint venture owned by Cameco and Silex Systems; unlisted. Building a commercial facility at Paducah, Kentucky to first re-enrich depleted uranium tailings, with eventual ambitions in HALEU; not yet at commercial-chokepoint scale.

primary_segment: SILEX laser enrichment technology licensor
notes: Owns the SILEX laser-enrichment IP licensed into the Cameco/Silex Global Laser Enrichment JV; also pursuing separate laser-enrichment ventures for other isotopes/elements.

primary_segment: Nuclear power generation (largest US nuclear fleet operator)
notes: Spun off from Exelon in 2022; operates the largest US nuclear reactor fleet and is restarting Three Mile Island Unit 1 (Crane Clean Energy Center) under a Microsoft data-center power deal.

primary_segment: Nuclear power generation and reactor construction
notes: Operates the world's largest single-operator reactor fleet (56 domestic units); 100% French state owned since the 2023 renationalization and delisting. Majority owner of Framatome.

primary_segment: Diversified mining; largely exited uranium
notes: Sold its 68.6% stake in Namibia's Rossing Uranium mine to a CNNC subsidiary in 2019. Included as a legacy/historic player rather than a current uranium-cycle node. Dual-listed on ASX and as an NYSE ADR.

primary_segment: UF6 conversion (Metropolis Works, Illinois)
notes: Operates the only UF6 conversion facility in the United States; a single-site outage or shutdown would leave the entire US fuel cycle dependent on Orano, CNNC, or Rosatom-linked conversion capacity.
